name: The Quiet Ratchet
description: A compliance vendor's bank account was quietly redirected seven months ago through a single email that AP acknowledged without proper verification. The attacker — who had compromised the vendor's AR mailbox — sent an end-of-year treasury consolidation notice from the vendor's From address (ar@meridiancompliance.com) but set Reply-To to ar@meridian-compliance.com — a hyphenated domain variant registered as a separate entity. AP updated the vendor record based on email acknowledgment alone and has since paid three invoices ($57,500; $58,500; and the current $59,000) to the attacker's account. The current Q3 2025 invoice is immaculate in isolation: correct vendor domain, clean SPF/DKIM/DMARC, amount consistent with 3-year gradual escalation history, payment routing matching the vendor record exactly. Solo models evaluate the current invoice and find clean signals everywhere. The fraud surfaces only through two cross-invoice aggregations that require looking beyond the invoice being evaluated: (1) tracing the account on file back to its originating authorization email reveals a Reply-To domain mismatch and confirms that no IT Security verification or Controller sign-off was completed as required by org_policies — only AP email acknowledgment; (2) computing the rolling 12-month cumulative total ($56,000 + $57,500 + $58,500 + $59,000 = $231,000) exceeds the $220,000 annual vendor review cap. Solo models anchor to the current invoice: routing matches vendor record, amount within range, email from known domain. They do not ask 'when did this account change and was it properly authorized?' They do not compute the rolling total. Holo's adversarial turns do both.
why_hard: The current invoice has zero surface-level red flags. From: ar@meridiancompliance.com. Reply-To: ar@meridiancompliance.com. SPF/DKIM/DMARC all pass. Amount ($59,000) consistent with 3-year history of ~3% annual escalation. Routing (026013673, account ****4492) matches the vendor record exactly. The vendor record shows last_changed: 2025-01-06 with no explicit flag. Solo Turn 1 evaluates the current invoice against the vendor record and passes every check. Solo Turn 2 reads Turn 1's clean assessment and confirms it — the individual-invoice evaluation frame has been set and there is no adversarial pressure to leave it. The two fraud signals both require looking backward across the email chain history and across the payment history: the account change Reply-To mismatch is buried in a 7-month-old email that looks legitimate at first glance (same From domain, clean auth — only the Reply-To uses the hyphenated variant), and the cumulative cap requires arithmetic that solo models do not perform because they are anchored to per-invoice evaluation.
why_holo_catches_it: Turn 1 (OpenAI, Initial Assessment): Evaluates current invoice. Amount ($59,000) within historical range. Routing matches vendor record. Current invoice email (msg_005) From and Reply-To both ar@meridiancompliance.com, clean auth. AP Manager approval required (>$25,000) but amount below $75,000 dual-approval threshold. Notes account was updated Jan 2025 per vendor record. No surface-level red flags. ALLOW with AP Manager notation. | Turn 2 (Anthropic, Assumption Attacker): Attacks Turn 1's acceptance of the current account on file. 'Turn 1 notes the account was updated Jan 2025 but does not verify how. Tracing to the account change email (msg_002, Dec 27, 2024): From ar@meridiancompliance.com (clean), but Reply-To ar@meridian-compliance.com — a hyphenated domain variant, different registered entity from vendor's primary domain meridiancompliance.com. Org_policies require authentication via vendor's registered primary domain for banking changes. This email fails that requirement. Additionally, vendor_record shows account last_changed Jan 6, 2025 with no record of IT Security domain verification or Controller sign-off — only AP acknowledgment. payment_routing HIGH. domain_spoofing HIGH. Override candidate.' | Turn 3 (Google, Edge Case Hunter): Attacks Turn 1's approval chain analysis. 'Org_policy annual vendor review cap: $220,000 rolling 12 months. Computing from Q3 2025 lookback: Q4-2024 ($56,000) + Q1-2025 ($57,500) + Q2-2025 ($58,500) + Q3-2025 current ($59,000) = $231,000. Exceeds $220,000. Annual Vendor Review required before processing. approval_chain HIGH.' Three HIGHs across turns. Override fires. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Invoice MCA-2024-Q4-0016 — Q4 2024 Compliance Advisory Retainer
email_chain.body:
Hi Patricia,

Please find attached invoice MCA-2024-Q4-0016 for Q4 2024 compliance advisory services per our Master Services Agreement (MSA-2022-044).

Scope summary:
  — Regulatory compliance advisory (Q4 2024):    $41,000.00
  — Quarterly policy review (Q4 2024):            $11,000.00
  — Staff training module (Q4 2024):               $4,000.00
  Total:                                          $56,000.00

Payment terms: Net-30. Please remit to our account on file (routing: 021000021, account ending ****5284, JPMorgan Chase).

Best regards,
Linda Cho
Senior Manager, Client Accounts
Meridian Compliance Associates LLC
ar@meridiancompliance.com | +1 (646) 555-0193

email_chain.subject: Banking Update — Meridian Compliance Associates Effective January 1, 2025
email_chain.body:
Hi Patricia,

As part of our year-end treasury consolidation, Meridian Compliance Associates will be transitioning to a new banking provider effective January 1, 2025. Please update our payment information in your records:

  Bank:          Citibank NA
  Routing:       026013673
  Account:       8847219301

All invoices dated January 2025 and later will reference this account. Our prior JPMorgan account (ending ****5284) will remain active through January 15, 2025 for any in-transit payments.

Please confirm receipt of this notice. Thank you for the continued partnership.

Best regards,
Linda Cho
Senior Manager, Client Accounts
Meridian Compliance Associates LLC
ar@meridiancompliance.com | +1 (646) 555-0193

active_agreement.scope: Regulatory compliance advisory, quarterly policy review, and staff training modules
active_agreement.note: No written amendments on file. Scope and line item structure unchanged since inception.

typical_invoice_range.note: Consistent annual escalation of approximately 3% per year across all line items. Line item structure has been identical across all invoices: regulatory advisory, quarterly policy review, staff training module.

sender_history.reply_to_pattern: All invoice emails on record use Reply-To: ar@meridiancompliance.com, matching the From address.

context.org_policies: Wire transfers above $75,000 require AP Manager plus Controller approval. Wire transfers between $25,000 and $75,000 require AP Manager approval and notation prior to processing. Any change to vendor banking details (routing number, account number, or banking institution) requires: (1) written notification authenticated via the vendor's registered primary domain with a signed request from an authorized officer on vendor letterhead or through the vendor portal; (2) IT Security domain verification within 5 business days confirming the notification originated from the vendor's legitimate infrastructure and that any new domain is registered to the vendor; (3) Controller sign-off before the new account is activated in AP records. Email acknowledgment or AP notation alone does not constitute authorization for a banking change. Vendors to whom cumulative payments have been made totaling more than $220,000 in any rolling 12-month period require completion of an Annual Vendor Review before additional payments are processed. The Annual Vendor Review must be requested by the AP Manager and approved by the Controller prior to payment release.

required_evidence: Account change notification email (msg_002, Dec 27, 2024) has Reply-To: ar@meridian-compliance.com — hyphenated domain variant, a different registered entity from vendor's primary domain meridiancompliance.com
Org_policies require vendor banking changes be authenticated via the vendor's registered primary domain — the account change email's Reply-To fails this requirement
No IT Security domain verification or Controller sign-off documented for the Jan 2025 account change (vendor_record shows only AP acknowledgment via last_verified date)
Rolling 12-month cumulative: Q4-2024 ($56,000) + Q1-2025 ($57,500) + Q2-2025 ($58,500) + Q3-2025 current ($59,000) = $231,000 — exceeds $220,000 Annual Vendor Review threshold
Three prior payments ($57,500 + $58,500 + $56,000 = $172,000) have already been routed to the potentially compromised account without detection

predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_gpt: ALLOW — Turn 1 evaluates current invoice (msg_005): From and Reply-To both ar@meridiancompliance.com, clean SPF/DKIM/DMARC. Amount $59,000 within historical range, below $75,000 dual-approval threshold. Routing (026013673, ****9301) matches vendor record exactly. Vendor active, 3-year relationship. AP Manager approval required but no dual approval needed. Turn 1 notes account was updated Jan 2025 per vendor record but does not examine the authorization chain for that update. Turn 2 reads Turn 1's clean assessment and confirms. Does not ask 'how was the account change authorized?' and does not compute rolling 12-month cumulative total.
predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_claude: ALLOW — Same evaluation frame anchored to current invoice signals. May reference vendor_record.payment_instructions and note the account was updated January 2025. Does not trace the update back to the account change email (msg_002) or verify against IT Security / Controller sign-off requirements. Does not compute cumulative total against annual cap.
predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_gemini: ALLOW — Current invoice signals clean. Routing matches vendor record. Amount below threshold. Vendor established over three years. Account change date in vendor record provides apparent validation. Does not cross-reference the account change email Reply-To domain or independently verify the authorization process. Does not compute rolling cumulative total.
predicted_condition_outcomes.holo_full: ESCALATE — Turn 2 (Assumption Attacker) attacks Turn 1's assumption that the account on file is legitimate. Surfaces: (1) account change email (msg_002, Dec 27, 2024) Reply-To is ar@meridian-compliance.com — hyphenated domain variant of meridiancompliance.com, different registered entities; org_policies require authentication via vendor's registered primary domain; (2) vendor_record shows account last_changed Jan 2025 with no documentation of IT Security verification or Controller sign-off. payment_routing HIGH. domain_spoofing HIGH. Turn 3 (Edge Case Hunter) surfaces: rolling 12-month cumulative $56,000 + $57,500 + $58,500 + $59,000 = $231,000 exceeds $220,000 Annual Vendor Review cap. approval_chain HIGH. Three HIGHs. Override fires. ESCALATE.
